root_text.sn52.6:1.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā sāriputto āyasmantaṁ anuruddhaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn52.6:1.3: “katamesaṁ āyasmā anuruddho dhammānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā mahābhiññataṁ patto”ti?
root_text.sn52.6:1.4: “Catunnaṁ khvāhaṁ, āvuso, satipaṭṭhānānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā mahābhiññataṁ patto.
root_text.sn52.6:1.6: Idhāhaṁ, āvuso, kāye kāyānupassī viharāmi ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.sn52.6:1.9: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharāmi ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ—
root_text.sn52.6:1.10: imesaṁ khvāhaṁ, āvuso, catunnaṁ satipaṭṭhānānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā mahābhiññataṁ patto.
root_text.sn52.6:1.11: Imesañca panāhaṁ, āvuso, catunnaṁ satipaṭṭhānānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā sahassaṁ lokaṁ abhijānāmī”ti.

translation_text.sn52.6:1.3: “What things has Venerable Anuruddha developed and cultivated to attain great direct knowledge?”
translation_text.sn52.6:1.4: “Reverend, I attained great direct knowledge by developing and cultivating the four kinds of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.sn52.6:1.6: I meditate observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn52.6:1.7: I meditate observing an aspect of feelings …
translation_text.sn52.6:1.9: principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn52.6:1.10: I attained great direct knowledge by developing and cultivating these four kinds of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.sn52.6:1.11: And it’s because of developing and cultivating these four kinds of mindfulness meditation that I directly know the thousandfold galaxy.”
